okamoto_constraint_mismatch.observation: CT v1.3 Thm:vquad-cc identifies the V_quad parameter point as (1/6, 0, 0, -1/2). These four numbers sum to -1/3, which does NOT satisfy the Okamoto constraint alpha_inf + alpha_0 + beta_inf + beta_0 = 0 quoted in the relay-prompt brief.
interpretation_options: (a) CT v1.3 uses a non-Okamoto convention (e.g. Sakai E_7^(1)/D_7^(1) root data), in which case the four parameters are not constrained to sum to zero;
(b) one of the four entries is a spectral-type label rather than a Hamiltonian parameter;
(c) the relay-prompt's quoted constraint is for a different P_III(D_6) parametrization than the one CT v1.3 uses.
okamoto_constraint_mismatch.action: Flagged as residual R1 in literature_crosscheck.md; do not select between (a)-(c) without Okamoto 1987 §2.
okamoto_constraint_mismatch.severity: informational --- does not block G15 partial closure but does block the final convention pinning.

scalar_ode_versus_Lax_pair_layer_separat.observation: V_quad's scalar OGF ODE is linear (Hamiltonization gives a quadratic-in-p Hamiltonian). Canonical P_III(D_6) is genuinely nonlinear (quadratic-in-p but with q-p coupling). The two Hamiltonians live at different layers: scalar L-equation versus isomonodromic deformation thereof. This was implicit in CT v1.3's framing 'algebraic identity at Painleve-class level only' but the layer structure is now made explicit in vquad_hamiltonian.tex's closing paragraph and in phi_change_of_variables.tex.
scalar_ode_versus_Lax_pair_layer_separat.implication: Phi cannot be a direct change-of-variables on (f, f', z); it must act on Lax-pair monodromy data. This sharpens the G15 statement and the residual list (R5 is the primary blocker, not R1-R4).
scalar_ode_versus_Lax_pair_layer_separat.severity: structural insight --- recommend Claude review whether CT v1.4 should rephrase §3.5 status from 'algebraic identity DIAGNOSED' to 'algebraic identity DIAGNOSED, with isomonodromic-correspondence statement still PENDING (R5)'.
